---
title: "Fragrant Flatsedge (Cyperus odoratus)"
slug: "cyperus-odoratus"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474891852/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Fragrant Flatsedge"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Cyperaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Cyperaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474891852/medium.jpg" alt="Fragrant Flatsedge" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/saherron">
saherron</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/264459562">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Cyperaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=63329)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in moist to wet habitats.

## Natural history notes

A grass-like sedge characterized by its triangular stems, narrow leaves, and umbrella-like clusters of spikelets. In Florida, it is commonly found in moist to wet habitats such as marshes, ditches, and disturbed wetlands. It grows during the warmer months and can form patches in open, seasonally wet areas, sometimes producing a noticeable scent when crushed.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/63329-Cyperus-odoratus)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/2790)
